text.title: BABOK KA1 Five Tasks
text.summary: Five BABOK KA1 tasks, ordering constraints, approach selection rules, stakeholder categories, and elicitation technique selection criteria.

rule.statement: Produce five separate task-scoped artifacts (T1 through T5), not one consolidated BA plan; each artifact has its own approver, cadence, and lifecycle.
rule.rationale: Collapsing five tasks into one document loses the monitoring half (T5) and makes the governance and information management tasks implicitly optional — they are not.

rule.statement: Execute KA1 tasks in sequence: T1 approach → T2 stakeholders → T3 governance → T4 information management → T5 performance; dependencies flow forward.
rule.rationale: Governance (T3) depends on knowing who the stakeholders are (T2); information management (T4) depends on governance decisions (T3); performance metrics (T5) depend on knowing what artifacts exist (T4). Out-of-order execution produces incoherent plans.

list.item: T1 — Plan BA Approach: approach selection (plan-driven/change-driven/hybrid), methodology, constraints, rationale. One named approver required.
T2 — Plan Stakeholder Engagement: stakeholder list, categories, engagement technique per type, availability.
T3 — Plan BA Governance: decision rights, change control process, approval thresholds, escalation paths, sign-off requirements.
T4 — Plan BA Information Management: artifact repository path, retention policy, access list, versioning approach.
T5 — Identify BA Performance Improvements: at least 3 measurable metrics listed on day one even with null baselines and targets.

rule.statement: Declare the approach as a combination of plan-driven and change-driven percentages per artifact, not as a binary choice.
rule.rationale: Real programs are 70/30 hybrids; pure-binary approach outputs produce artifacts that are either over-planned or under-baselined for their actual volatility.

list.item: Plan-Driven indicators: clear scope, stable requirements, high documentation expectation, low change frequency, limited stakeholder availability.
Change-Driven indicators: uncertain evolving requirements, continuous stakeholder availability, higher risk tolerance, "just enough" documentation norm.
Sponsors: executive funding the initiative — decision authority for scope and budget changes.
Users: people who will use the solution — primary source for functional and usability requirements.
Implementers: team building the solution — source for feasibility and technical constraints.
Regulators: compliance bodies — source for mandatory requirements and audit expectations.
Support: operations and help desk — source for operational and transition requirements.

antipattern.description: Listing plausible-sounding stakeholder roles without confirming they exist in the org chart.
antipattern.reason: LLMs invent plausible stakeholders ("Director of UX," "VP of Platform"); gap to reality causes missed sign-offs and broken escalation paths at critical decision points.

rule.statement: Select elicitation techniques by information type and stakeholder count, not by default preference; document the technique-to-session mapping before any session is scheduled.
rule.rationale: Agents default to "interview everything" regardless of context; workshops are more efficient for group consensus, surveys for broad input across large stakeholder populations.

list.item: Interviews: deep individual understanding, SME knowledge extraction, 1-3 participants.
Workshops: consensus building, requirements that need group negotiation, 4-15 participants.
Surveys: broad input, many stakeholders, quantitative preference data, 15+ respondents.
Observation: process understanding, gap between documented and actual behavior, 1-2 observers.
Document Analysis: existing-state artifacts, regulatory documents, system specifications.

text.reference: BA approach document covering all six required sections.
Python script validating five KA1 artifacts are present and within review cadence.
